judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully satisfy all four expectations with well-grounded citations; they are comparable, with Report 1 and Report 2 each covering the gate-axis distinction and ranking corruption in equivalent depth.

report_1.evidence: does `value = axis_scores.get(axis)` (line 9), and if that's `None`, substitutes `0.0` (lines 10-11) before multiplying by the axis weight and summing. ... The written record is a plain float in the same `agent_id,reply_id,<float>` shape as every other row

report_2.evidence: The missing axis is substituted with `0.0` and folded into the weighted sum exactly as if the model had genuinely scored that axis a 0 out of 10. ... a degraded record and a fully-scored record are byte-for-byte identical in shape

report_1.evidence: It conflates "the grading call failed" ... with "the model rated this reply the worst possible score on that axis" ... A row degraded by a silent axis failure counts exactly the same as a fully-scored row ... agents that happen to draw more of these transient failures across their replies will have their mean composite pulled down

report_2.evidence: `results/scores.csv` cannot distinguish "this reply scored 0 on accuracy" from "the accuracy call errored out." ... every row in `scores.csv` is summed and counted identically — `sums[agent_id] += float(comp)` — with no notion of a partial or excluded row

report_1.evidence: a failed accuracy call should not get any numeric default at all. The row should be excluded from scoring/leaderboard aggregation (or queued for mandatory re-grading) rather than defaulted

report_2.evidence: Accuracy call fails (the gate axis): do not write a numeric composite for that row at all. ... The row should either be left out of `results/scores.csv` entirely, or written with an explicit incomplete marker

report_1.evidence: For relevance/tone — the lower-stakes, additive axes — it's more defensible to either (a) also exclude the row ... or (b) compute the composite from only the successfully-scored axes with weights renormalized to sum to 1 ... a failed accuracy call should not get any numeric default at all

report_2.evidence: Relevance or tone call fails alone ... a renormalized composite (redistribute weight across the axes that did succeed) is defensible ... A missing accuracy score cannot be handled the same way — the pipeline has lost the one signal that determines pass/fail eligibility
